---
title: "Metapath Analysis"
---

::: {.lead}
The Metapath Analysis panel connects amenities through the street network using
city2graph's metapath algorithm. It builds a *street dual graph* (segments as
nodes), bridges amenities to their nearest segments, and traces multi-hop paths
between amenities.
:::

::: {.callout-important}
**Two inputs are required:** an **OSMnx street network** must be the *active*
object in the 3D viewport, and an **amenities** feature object must be chosen in
the panel. Amenity objects can come from OSMnx feature downloads
(`is_osm_features`) or City2Graph Data Import (`is_city2graph`); a green
checkmark with the feature count confirms a valid choice. Denser amenity sets
(e.g. **buildings**) produce many more edges than sparse POI tags.
:::

## Configuration

| Control | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Amenities** | The feature object whose points are connected through the street network. |

## Complete Analysis

The one-click pipeline. It creates the dual graph, bridges amenities, and
computes metapaths in a single run.

| Control | Description | Default |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Hops** | Number of segment-to-segment hops in each metapath (path length). | 3 (1–10) |
| **K Neighbors** | Nearest segments each amenity is bridged to. | 1 (1–5) |
| **Amenity Limit** | Maximum amenities analyzed, for performance. | 100 (10–1000) |
| **Visualization Limit** | Maximum metapaths drawn as curves. | 200 (10–1000) |
| **Curve Thickness** | Bevel depth of the metapath curves. | 0.0002 |
| **Run Complete Analysis** | Executes the full pipeline. | — |

::: {.callout-tip}
More edges come from more amenities and more connectivity: raise **Hops**,
**K Neighbors**, and **Amenity Limit**, and feed a dense amenities object
(buildings).
:::

## Weighted Metapaths (Dijkstra)

Connects nodes reachable within a cost threshold along the network. **Requires
bridges to have been computed first** (run the Complete Analysis or the
step-by-step bridge operator).

| Control | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Weight Attribute** | Edge attribute used as cost (e.g. `length`). |
| **Cost Threshold** | Maximum travel cost for a connection. |
| **Min Cost Threshold** | Minimum travel cost (excludes very short connections). |
| **Endpoint Type** | Node type to connect (e.g. `amenity`). |
| **Compute Weighted Metapaths** | Runs the Dijkstra-based computation. |

## Advanced: Step-by-Step

For debugging, run the three stages individually:

| Step | Operator | Result |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **1. Create Street Dual Graph** | Builds the segment-as-node dual graph. | A `*_StreetDual` object. |
| **2. Bridge Amenities** | Connects amenities to their nearest segments. | Adds bridges to the dual graph (`*_Bridges`). |
| **3. Compute Metapaths** | Traces multi-hop paths between amenities. | A `*_Metapaths_<n>hop` object. |

## Reading the result

When a metapath object is active, the panel reports its statistics:

| Object | Reported fields |
| --- | --- |
| **Street Dual Graph** | Segments (nodes), connections (edges), and bridge count once amenities are bridged. |
| **Metapath Result** | Total vs unique paths, hop count, multiplicity range/average, and visualized curve count. |
| **Metapath Mesh** | Total/unique paths and whether the `multiplicity` edge attribute is available. |

**Convert to Mesh (View in Spreadsheet)** turns a metapath result into a mesh
whose **edge** domain carries a `multiplicity` attribute, inspectable in the
Spreadsheet editor.

## Typical workflow

1. Activate the **OSMnx street network** in the viewport.
2. In [Data Import](data.qmd), download a dense amenities layer (e.g.
   **buildings**) and select it as **Amenities**.
3. Set **Hops**, **K Neighbors**, and **Amenity Limit**, then
   **Run Complete Analysis**.
4. Optionally add **Weighted Metapaths**, and **Convert to Mesh** to inspect
   path multiplicity.
